location.name: Kaniq Creek
location.level_note: Just unlocked at Level 38 (2026-04-13). Next progression destination after Saint-Croix and SJD.

primary_targets: Chinook Salmon (highest value salmon, trophy class)
Northern Pike (TITANIUM LEADER REQUIRED for bite-off protection)

loadout_strategy.philosophy: Adapted from current SJD loadout. Kaniq Creek is a SALMON RIVER - active spoon/spinner fishing dominates. NOT a heavy-bottom location like Saint-Croix or SJD. Pike presence REQUIRES Titanium leaders on all active rods (cannot use Fluoro). Reduce bottom rods from 4 (sturgeon) to 1-2 (sucker/bull trout); convert remaining bottom slots to active salmon rods.
key_changes_from_sjd_loadout: CRITICAL: Swap Fluorocarbon leaders to TITANIUM on slots 1, 2, 3 (Northern Pike present - bite-off risk)
Slots 4-7: REDUCE bottom rods from 4 to 1-2; convert others to active salmon casting/spinning
Bait change: Salmon Eggs / Roe replace Large Cutbait on the remaining bottom rods
Lure focus: Spoons (Single Spoon 1.5 Oz, Narrow Spoon 3/4 Oz, Salmon-color spoons) and Spinners
Skip the 4-Rod Stand setup - not appropriate for salmon river fishing style

slots.rod: GalaxyCast 8'6" (Casting Rod, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz, 7-28.6 Lb, Mod Fast)
slots.setup_type: Casting Jig + Soft Plastic / Egg Cluster
slots.notes: Long-range casting for salmon runs. Titanium leader handles pike bite-off. Try Salmon Egg Cluster on jighead for spawning salmon.

slots.rod: Silverthorn Stake 7'6" (Casting Rod, X-Heavy, 7/8-3 1/6 Oz, 35.3-88 Lb)
slots.leader: Titanium Leader .025" (NEW - swap from direct fluoro for pike protection)
slots.notes: PRIMARY SALMON HUNT MISSION ROD continues at Kaniq Creek. Same setup that Day 1 SJD couldn't find a 33+ lb chinook - try Alaska river runs which may produce bigger fish. CRITICAL: Add Titanium leader for pike bite-off.

slots.rod: Rivertex Salmonster 8'10" (Spinning, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz, 18-46 Lb, Mod Fast)
slots.notes: Salmonster + spoon = proven cross-location salmon producer. Glow&Holo spoon works in low-light Alaska conditions. Drop-and-pause technique transfers well to salmon.

slots.rod: GalaxyCast 5'11" SE (Casting, X-Heavy, 7/8-2.5 Oz) - Supernatural Pack
slots.notes: REPURPOSED FROM SJD: previously Heavy Chaser bottom (Crawfish/sturgeon). At Kaniq, swap to short casting rod + Spinnerbait for active pike fishing along weed edges and slow pools. Spinnerbait + Titanium = standard pike/salmon combo.

slots.rod: Salmonster 8'2" (Spinning, X-Heavy, 5/8-2 Oz, 15-42 Lb, Mod Fast)
slots.notes: REPURPOSED FROM SJD: previously Humenus bottom (Large Cutbait/sturgeon). At Kaniq, swap to lighter spinning + Narrow Spoon for char and smaller salmon. Drop-pause technique works for Bull Trout and Dolly Varden in deep pools.

slots.rod: Mokonzi 12'10" SE (Feeder, X-Heavy, 6-9 7/8 Oz, 39.7-81.6 Lb, Moderate)
slots.terminal_tackle: Open Feeder 4 1/2 Oz + Mix Grand Fish Feeder, Hook #2
slots.bait: Salmon Eggs / Roe / Salmon Egg Cluster
slots.notes: REPURPOSED FROM SJD: previously sturgeon feeder (Large Cutbait). At Kaniq, swap to Salmon Eggs / Roe under feeder for spawning runs - this is the STANDARD Alaska salmon drift technique. No Titanium leader needed (no pike on bottom drift). Smaller hook (#2) for delicate salmon mouths.

REPURPOSED FROM SJD: previously sturgeon bottom (Large Cutbait). At Kaniq, single bottom rod with Night Crawlers for Longnose Sucker (panfish completion) and Bull Trout/Dolly Varden bycatch. Heaviest bottom rod kept for any large char that hit. Could swap to Roe if salmon are running through the deep pools.

baits_to_buy: Salmon Eggs - PRIMARY salmon spawning bait
Salmon Egg Cluster - tournament-grade salmon bait
Shrimp - bull trout, dolly varden alternative
lures_to_consider: Pink/Orange Salmon spoons - Coho/Sockeye matching colors
Glow&Holo Medium Spoon 1 Oz #4/0 (already have - low-light champion)
Spinnerbait 3/4-1 Oz - pike + salmon combo lure
Bullet Spinner 1-1.25 Oz - confirmed at SJD for chinook

first_trip: Buy unlimited Alaska License (cost TBD - check at travel)
Visit Angling the Middle-Earth first - shore river bend with salmon traffic
Deploy active rods (slots 1, 2, 3) with spoons for incoming salmon runs
Slot 6 (Mokonzi feeder + Salmon Eggs) anchors a passive drift line
Rotate to Hunter's Cabin for pike + char variety
Try Taming the Kaniq for fast-water salmon
Goal Day 1: catch one of each of the 5 salmon species + Bull Trout + Dolly Varden + Northern Pike
income_priorities: Chinook Salmon = highest individual value among salmon
Bull Trout = char family trophy income (similar to lake trout pricing)
Coho Salmon = volume Pacific salmon income
Pink/Chum Salmon = lower individual value but volume runs
missions_to_check: Salmon Hunt (Spoon Lure) - if cross-location, Kaniq Chinook may be easier than SJD
Pacific Salmon collection missions (Coho, Sockeye, Pink, Chum trophies)
Bull Trout / Dolly Varden mastering missions
Kaniq Creek Exploration I (10 species - knock out on first trip)
Look for a Kaniq-specific Fish Monster mission

critical_warnings: NORTHERN PIKE PRESENT - all active lure rods MUST use Titanium leader (Fluoro will be bitten off)
Salmon Hunt mission MAY transfer here - 33+ lb Chinook potentially easier in Alaska river vs SJD California
Cold-weather location - check if license type affects gear durability differently
Spawning runs are seasonal in-game weather/time-dependent - plan around weather window for best salmon density

loadout_progress.improvement_summary: Field-tested 2026-04-13. Medium Spoon 6/7 Oz #6/0 on Silverthorn Stake = MVP (8+ salmon, Unique Chum 30.78 lb, Unique Sockeye 15.5 lb). Finesse retrieve technique critical.

kaniq_retrieve_technique.summary: Multi-stage zig-zag retrieve for Pacific salmon — SMOOTH movement, not jerky. Salmon hit almost exclusively on the DROP phase.
sequence: 2. Let drop SLOWLY to bottom (6/7 Oz spoon weight is correct drop rate)
3. Reel + raise slowly until lure reaches ~50% depth from bottom
8. Repeat cycle until lure approaches surface
9. Final drop to ~70-80% depth, then retrieve
key_principles: Salmon hit on the DROP phase, rarely on the raise
SMOOTH movement - twitches and jerks shut down the bite
Lure weight (6/7 Oz) controls drop speed - lighter/heavier breaks the cadence
Shallow + rocky waterways = high snag risk on bottom proximity stages

overall_analysis.user_verdict: MIXED FEELINGS. Technical finesse fishing is fun but XP/income underwhelming vs Saint-Croix and SJD. Tabled for later date - not returning immediately. Good for mission completion but not income farming.
